classes.name: processing.common.TimelineWalker.TimelineWalker
documentation:
The walker for timelines

Feed multiple timelines to enumerate their intervals.

The name can be misleading as this class takes multiple timelines as input.
This behaviour allows keeping track of loaded files and extracted data (in
order to do it once per file and extractor).

classes.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.settings
documentation:
The information gathered from the configuration file.

This will contain multiple groups and datasets corresponding to each tab
of the user Excel configuration file.

User will need to refresh using `pnpm process:config` to update data located
inside the storage file.

Type:
    Group

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.bands_names
documentation:
The names of specified bands.

Each row contains a string.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.bands_lows
documentation:
The low frequencies for each band.

Each row contains one integer for the frequency in Hertz.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.bands_highs
documentation:
The high frequencies for each band.

Each row contains one integer for the frequency in Hertz.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.integrations_names
documentation:
The names of specified integrations.

Each row contains a string.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.integrations_seconds
documentation:
The duration in seconds for each band.

Each row contains an integer.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.ranges_names
documentation:
The names of specified ranges.

Each row contains a string.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.ranges_starts
documentation:
The start date for each range.

Each row contains a timestamp as an integer.

Timestamps are in UNIX format and milliseconds.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.ranges_ends
documentation:
The end date for each range.

Each row contains a timestamp as an integer.

Timestamps are in UNIX format and milliseconds.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.files_names
documentation:
The file paths relative to the audio folder.

Each row contains a string.

Each string starts with a `/`.

This `/` is a convention because it gets merged with the audio folder path.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.files_sites
documentation:
The site for each file.

Each row contains a string.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.files_labels
documentation:
The labels for each file.

Each row contains `n` columns of strings.

Each column has the index of the corresponding label property.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.files_timestamps
documentation:
The timestamp for each file.

Timestamps are in UNIX format and milliseconds.
Each row contains an integer.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.files_durations
documentation:
The audio durations for each file.

Audio durations are in seconds.
Each row contains an integer.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.labels_properties
documentation:
The meta properties specified in the configuration file.

Each row contains a string.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.labels_sets
documentation:
The meta unique values (sets).

Each row contains a variable number of strings (separated as columns).

Each string represent a possible value given
the meta property (referenced by its index).

Meta sets are filled with empty strings `""` to keep the dataset rectangular.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.autoclusters_names
documentation:
The names of autoclusters requested in the configuration file.

Each row is a string referring to a particular algorithm.

Enumeration:
    ../clusterings/ClusteringName.py

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.autoclusters_min_cluster_sizes
documentation:
The minimum count of elements within a given cluster.

Each row contains an integer.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.autoclusters_min_samples
documentation:
The minimum samples for autoclustering algorithms.

Each row contains an integer.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.autoclusters_alphas
documentation:
The alpha values for autoclustering algorithms.

Each row contains a float.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.autoclusters_epsilons
documentation:
The epsilon values for autoclustering algorithms.

Each row contains a float.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.trajectories_names
documentation:
The names of trajectories requested in the configuration file.

Each row is a string.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.trajectories_starts
documentation:
The start date for each trajectory.

Each row contains a timestamp as an integer.

Timestamps are in UNIX format and milliseconds.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.trajectories_ends
documentation:
The end date for each trajectory.

Each row contains a timestamp as an integer.

Timestamps are in UNIX format and milliseconds.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.reducers_names
documentation:
The names of reducers requested in the configuration file.

Each row contains a string.

Enumeration:
    ../reducers/ReducerName.py

Factory:
    ../reducers/Reducer.py

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.reducers_dimensions
documentation:
The dimensions for each reducer.

Each row contains an integer.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.reducers_bands
documentation:
The bands for each reducer.

Each row contains an array of strings (stored as columns) corresponding
to the name of the band.

Rows are filled with empty strings `""` if needed to keep the dataset rectangular.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.reducers_integrations
documentation:
The integration names for each reducer.

Each row contains an array of strings (stored as columns) corresponding
to the name of the integration.

Rows are filled with empty strings `""` if needed to keep the dataset rectangular.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.reducers_ranges
documentation:
The range names for each reducer.

Each row contains an array of strings (stored as columns) corresponding
to the name of the range.

Rows are filled with empty strings `""` if needed to keep the dataset rectangular.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.pairings_names
documentation:
The pairing names requested in the configuration file.

Each row contains a string.

Enumeration:
    ../pairings/PairingName.py

Factory:
    ../pairings/Pairing.py

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.computation_umap
documentation:
The computation UMAPs needed for further data generation.

Computation UMAPs are used for:
    - Mean distances matrix
    - Trajectories

The number of computation UMAPs is specified in the configuration file.

The group contains one dataset per computation UMAP index (iteration),
band and integration value.

Each dataset is named after the integration value.

Each row contains an array (columns) of floats.

This path is to be used as a prefix!

Example:
    /computation_umap_{computation_umap_index}/{band}/{integration}

Type:
    Group prefix

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.mean_distances_matrix
documentation:
The mean distances matrix for further data generation.

Mean distances matrices are used for:
    - Autoclustering

The group contains one dataset per band and integration value.
Each dataset is named after the integration value.
Each row contains an array (columns) of floats.

Example:
    /mean_distances_matrix/{band}/{integration}

Type:
    Group

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.sites_names
documentation:
The unique site names found in the project.

Each row contains a string.

Type:
    Dataset

class_variables.name: processing.storage.StoragePath.StoragePath.sites_file_indexes
documentation:
The file indexes for each site.

Each row contains one or more integers representing file indexes.

The number of file indexes per site can varry.
Making rectangular for storage and trimming for reconstructing is therefore needed.

Type:
    Dataset

The paths for all data located in the storage file.

File type:
    HDF

Details:
    https://www.wikiwand.com/en/Hierarchical_Data_Format

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: processing.lib.ENES_index.torch_leq
documentation:
computes the Leq Tensor from a torch Tensor such as given by torchaudio.load
waveform, sample_rate = torchaudio.load(file_path)

Parameters
----------
waveform : torchTensor[nb_channels, nb_samples_per_channels]
    raw wav samples
sample_rate : int
    sample rate in Hz
dt : float
    integration time for the Leq values in seconds

Returns
-------
Leq torch.Tensor[nb_channels, nb_Leq _values]

functions.name: processing.lib.ENES_index.numpy_leq
documentation:
computes the Leq np.array from a np.array such as given by
scipy.io.wavfile.read
import scipy.io.wavfile as wavfile
sample_rate, waveform = wavfile.read(file_path)

Parameters
----------
waveform : np.array[nb_samples_per_channels, nb_channels]
    raw wav samples
sample_rate : int
    sample rate in Hz
dt : float
    integration time for the Leq values in seconds

Returns
-------
Leq np.array[nb_channels, nb_Leq _values]

functions.name: processing.lib.ENES_index.energy_fract_from_spect
documentation:
Parameters
----------
waveform : np.array[nb_samples_per_channels, nb_channels]
    raw wav samples
sample_rate : int
    sample rate in Hz
dt : float
    integration time for the fourier mean transform

Returns
-------
cumFourier : np.array[nb_of_frequency, nb_channels]
    the cumulative sum of the mean spectrum power over the frequency axis
freq : np.array[nb_of_frequency]
    the corresponding frequency values

functions.name: processing.utils.is_within_interval.is_within_interval
documentation:
Check if data timestamps fall within the specified interval.

Determines whether the given data's start and end timestamps are contained
within the specified interval. The function will return `True` even if the
data partially overlaps with the interval.

It is essential to use the same time unit for all timestamps for consistent results.

Note:
    - The interval is defined by the `interval_start` (inclusive) and
      `interval_end` (exclusive).
    - The function will return `True` if the data spans across the interval
      boundaries.

Args:
    interval_start (int): The start timestamp of the interval.
    interval_end (int): The end timestamp of the interval.
    data_start (int): The start timestamp of the data.
    data_end (int): The end timestamp of the data.

Returns:
    `True` if any part of the data falls within the interval, `False` otherwise.

functions.name: processing.utils.convert_date_to_timestamp.convert_date_to_timestamp
documentation:
Converts a date to timestamp.

The resulting timestamp is in UNIX format and milliseconds.

Args:
    date_string: A pandas Timestamp object.

Returns:
    The timestamp in milliseconds as an integer.
